architectural_purpose: deterministic orchestration diagnostics and explainability aggregation without operational orchestration intelligence

diagnostics.message: Unsupported orchestration capabilities remain visible and unavailable.
Blocked orchestration states remain visible and do not trigger sequencing.
Prohibited orchestration capabilities remain fail-visible and inactive.
Missing execution metadata remains visible because execution metadata is prohibited.
Conflicting execution authorization metadata remains blocked and descriptive.
Stale v4.2 coordination evidence remains read-only ancestry.
Explainability summaries describe blocked, unsupported, and prohibited states deterministically.
Orchestration manifests remain non-authorizing and non-executing.
Governance boundaries are explicit and do not create operational behavior.
Topology identity is deterministic and non-executable.
Unsupported topology relationships remain visible and unavailable.
Blocked topology relationships remain visible and do not trigger traversal.
Prohibited topology relationships remain fail-visible and inactive.
Stale topology ancestry remains read-only evidence.
Conflicting authorization relationships remain blocked and descriptive.
Missing runtime execution metadata remains visible because execution is prohibited.
Topology explainability describes unavailable traversal, routing, dependency resolution, and execution.
Topology infrastructure remains non-executing, non-traversing, non-routing, and non-resolving.
Capability identity is deterministic and non-executable.
Prohibited capabilities remain visible and inactive.
Unsupported capabilities remain visible and unavailable.
Blocked orchestration activation capabilities remain non-operational.
Stale topology context capabilities remain read-only ancestry.
Conflicting authorization capabilities remain blocked by non-execution governance.
Capability relationships to boundaries, policies, topology, and manifest remain descriptive.
Capability explainability documents unavailable activation, execution, planner integration, and operations.
Enabled operational capability count remains zero.
Policy identity is modeled as deterministic descriptive governance evidence.
Unsupported policy classifications are fail-visible and not inferred into enforcement.
Blocked policy states remain descriptive and cannot activate orchestration behavior.
Stale policy context is visible without repair or correction.
Conflicting policy metadata is visible without authorization or mutation.
Policy enforcement execution is explicitly prohibited and fail-visible.
Policy relationships are visible as target evidence only and cannot route or traverse.
Policy visibility explicitly certifies enabled_policy_enforcement_count equals 0.
Policy visibility explicitly certifies enabled_operational_capability_count equals 0.
Transition identity is modeled as deterministic descriptive governance evidence.
Unsupported transition classifications are fail-visible and not inferred into runtime behavior.
Blocked transition states remain descriptive and cannot activate orchestration behavior.
Stale transition context is visible without repair or correction.
Conflicting transition metadata is visible without authorization or state progression.
Transition execution is explicitly prohibited and fail-visible.
Transition relationships are visible as governance evidence only and cannot route or traverse.
Transition visibility explicitly certifies enabled_transition_execution_count equals 0.
Transition visibility certifies operational capability and policy enforcement counts remain 0.
Coordination identity is modeled as deterministic descriptive governance evidence.
Unsupported coordination classifications are fail-visible and not inferred into runtime coordination.
Blocked coordination states remain descriptive and cannot activate orchestration behavior.
Stale coordination context is visible without repair or correction.
Conflicting coordination claims are visible without dispatch or authorization.
Operational coordination and orchestration dispatch are explicitly prohibited.
Coordination relationships are visible as governance evidence only and cannot dispatch or route.
Coordination visibility explicitly certifies enabled_coordination_execution_count equals 0.
Coordination visibility certifies transition, policy, and operational capability counts remain 0.
Cross-layer prohibited orchestration governance evidence is aggregated without recommendations, repair, or decisions.
Cross-layer unsupported orchestration governance evidence is aggregated without recommendations, repair, or decisions.
Cross-layer blocked orchestration governance evidence is aggregated without recommendations, repair, or decisions.
Cross-layer stale orchestration governance evidence is aggregated without recommendations, repair, or decisions.
Cross-layer conflicting orchestration governance evidence is aggregated without recommendations, repair, or decisions.
Continuity diagnostics are aggregated as replay-safe and rollback-safe evidence only.
Provenance diagnostics remain visible across layers without production consumption.
Lineage diagnostics remain visible across layers without planner integration.
Diagnostics aggregation certifies enabled coordination, transition, policy, capability, decision, and recommendation counts remain 0.
Diagnostics aggregation cannot recommend, rank, score, select, optimize, authorize, or decide orchestration actions.

explainability_summaries.summary: Operational orchestration activation is blocked because Phase 1 only models manifest governance evidence.
Future provider contracts are unsupported because no executable orchestration contract exists in this phase.
Runtime orchestration execution is prohibited by the non-execution governance boundary.

Routing, scheduling, sequencing, dependency resolution, remediation, repair, authorization, readiness approval, planner integration, production consumption, recommendation, ranking, scoring, and selection are unavailable.
Governance boundaries exist to keep orchestration evidence replay-safe, rollback-safe, provenance-continuous, lineage-continuous, and non-executable.
The topology is blocked for operational activation because Phase 2 models structure only.
Runtime execution relationships are prohibited by non-execution topology governance.
Future provider relationships are unsupported until a later approved governance phase.
Prior v4.2 coordination topology ancestry is stale read-only evidence, not a live source.
Execution authorization relationships conflict with the explicit non-execution boundary.
Topology traversal is unavailable because the topology is evidence, not an executable graph.
Routing is unavailable because relationship visibility does not select paths or routes.
Dependency resolution is unavailable because relationships are not resolvers.
Execution remains disabled across graph, runtime, routing, scheduling, and sequencing surfaces.
Runtime execution is prohibited because capabilities are governance evidence only.
Future runtime coordination is unsupported until explicitly authorized by a later phase.
Orchestration activation is blocked because Phase 3 does not activate capabilities.
Prior topology context is stale read-only ancestry, not an operational source.
Execution authorization conflicts with the explicit non-execution capability boundary.
Capability activation cannot occur because enabled operational capability count is zero.
Execution remains unavailable across orchestration, runtime, capability, routing, traversal, dependency, sequencing, and scheduling surfaces.
Planner integration remains unavailable because capability visibility does not alter planner behavior.
Operational orchestration remains prohibited without explicit authorization in a later phase.
Governance boundaries exist to keep capability evidence deterministic, replay-safe, rollback-safe, and non-executable.
A policy is prohibited when it appears to enable enforcement, execution, authorization, activation, or production consumption.
Unsupported policy classifications remain visible as future governance surfaces without inference or enforcement.
A policy is blocked when it would imply orchestration activation, which Phase 4 cannot perform.
Stale policy context is reported deterministically and cannot be repaired automatically.
Conflicting policy claims are fail-visible because authorization remains unavailable.
Policy enforcement remains unavailable because Phase 4 provides policy visibility only.
Authorization remains unavailable because policy models cannot approve readiness or execution.
Orchestration activation remains unavailable because policies are descriptive governance evidence.
Execution remains unavailable because no orchestration engine, policy engine, or activation pathway exists.
Planner integration remains unavailable because Phase 4 cannot consume or steer operational planning.
Production consumption remains unavailable because policy evidence is replay-safe and rollback-safe descriptive output.
Governance constraints exist to keep manifests, topology, capabilities, and boundaries auditable without operational control.
A transition is prohibited when it appears to enable transition execution, state progression, dispatch, or runtime behavior.
Unsupported transition states remain visible as future governance surfaces without inference or activation.
A transition is blocked when it would imply orchestration activation, which Phase 5 cannot perform.
Stale transition context is reported deterministically and cannot be repaired automatically.
Conflicting transition claims are fail-visible because state progression remains unavailable.
Transition execution remains unavailable because Phase 5 provides transition visibility only.
Orchestration activation remains unavailable because transitions are descriptive governance evidence.
State progression remains unavailable because no executable transition state machine exists.
Planner integration remains unavailable because Phase 5 cannot consume or steer operational planning.
Production consumption remains unavailable because transition evidence is replay-safe and rollback-safe descriptive output.
Governance constraints exist to keep theoretical transitions auditable without operational flow.
Operational orchestration remains prohibited because no transition engine, runtime, state machine, or dispatcher exists.

Operational coordination remains unavailable because Phase 6 provides coordination visibility only.
Orchestration dispatch remains unavailable because no dispatcher or coordination engine exists.
Orchestration activation remains unavailable because coordination records are descriptive governance evidence.
Planner integration remains unavailable because coordination visibility cannot steer operational planning.
Production consumption remains unavailable because coordination evidence is replay-safe and rollback-safe descriptive output.
Governance constraints exist to keep theoretical coordination auditable without operational runtime behavior.
Runtime coordination remains prohibited because no coordination engine, dispatcher, runtime coordinator, or operational state machine exists.
Orchestration remains non-executable because Phase 7 aggregates governance diagnostics only.
Orchestration activation remains unavailable because aggregated evidence cannot activate capabilities or transitions.
Runtime coordination remains unavailable because coordination evidence is descriptive and non-coordinating.
Planner integration remains unavailable because diagnostics aggregation cannot steer operational planning.

Orchestration recommendations remain prohibited because diagnostics aggregation does not rank, score, select, or optimize outcomes.

explicit_limitations: v4.3 Phase 7 creates deterministic orchestration diagnostics aggregation only.
v4.3 Phase 7 aggregates explainability before orchestration intelligence.
v4.3 Phase 7 does not execute orchestration.
v4.3 Phase 7 does not recommend orchestration actions.
v4.3 Phase 7 does not rank, score, select, or optimize orchestration states.
v4.3 Phase 7 does not make orchestration decisions.
v4.3 Phase 7 does not authorize orchestration or approve readiness.
v4.3 Phase 7 does not dispatch, activate, route, traverse, schedule, sequence, or resolve dependencies.
v4.3 Phase 7 does not remediate, repair, infer, mutate runtime state, or mutate operational state.
v4.3 Phase 7 does not integrate with planner systems or consume production bundles.
explicit_prohibitions: No orchestration intelligence engine may exist.
No orchestration recommendation engine may exist.
No orchestration decision engine may exist.
